message:
perf(runtime,sync): optimize scheduler counter decrements, parker unpark fast path, and semaphore lock acquisition

Three targeted performance improvements:

GlobalInjector counter decrements (global_injector.rs):
- Replace fetch_update CAS retry loops with a load-check + fetch_sub
  pattern for pending_count and ready_count decrements
- Since these counters are advisory (Relaxed ordering, used for
  heuristics/metrics rather than correctness), the non-atomic
  load + sub pair is acceptable and avoids CAS contention under
  high steal throughput
- Add detailed documentation explaining the trade-off

Parker::unpark fast path (worker.rs):
- Use atomic swap instead of store for the notified flag
- If swap returns true (already notified), skip the mutex acquisition
  and condvar signal entirely — this is the common case when wakers
  fire for already-runnable tasks
- Only acquire the mutex and signal when transitioning from not-notified
  to notified, which is the only case where the thread might be parked

Semaphore double-lock elimination (semaphore.rs):
- In both AcquireFuture and OwnedAcquireFuture, move the lock
  acquisition above the waiter_id allocation branch so that the
  waiter_id is allocated inside the same critical section as the
  subsequent state check
- Eliminates a lock-unlock-relock sequence on first poll, reducing
  mutex contention and avoiding a TOCTOU window between id allocation
  and state inspection

Steal test improvement (stealing.rs):
- Replace early return + panic with found flag + assert for cleaner
  test structure and better failure messages

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
